channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: MAGA civil war erupts between Musk, critics over H-1B visas
item.description: A Trump World civil war has erupted over visas for highly skilled workers, with the president-elect’s new tech industry allies like Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy on one side and the anti-immigration MAGA base on the other.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: Race to succeed Rep. Elise Stefanik in upstate New York heats up with new challenger
item.description: New York Republican State Sen. Dan Stec of North Country is running for Congress to succeed Rep. Elise Stefanik, who is expected to be the next U.S. ambassador to the United Nations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Court orders Biden admin to stop selling border wall materials, was 'illegally subverting' laws: Texas AG
item.description: The Biden administration has been ordered to stop selling off border materials ahead of the incoming Trump administration next month.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden White House to send $1.25 billion in weapon aid to Ukraine before Trump transition: report
item.description: The Biden White House is set to announce an additional $1.25 billion in military assistance to Ukraine as the incoming Trump administration to take office in January.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge signals that contempt hearing for Rudy Giuliani over his assets might not go well for him
item.description: A federal judge is signaling that Rudy Giuliani's contempt hearing next Friday might not end so well for the former New York City mayor and onetime personal lawyer for President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Larry Neumeister, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump asks Supreme Court to delay TikTok ban so he can weigh in after he takes office
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has asked the Supreme Court to pause the potential TikTok ban from going into effect until his administration can pursue a “political resolution” to the issue.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Janet Yellen tells Congress US could hit debt limit in mid-January
item.description: Treasury Secretary Janet Yellen says her agency will need to start taking “extraordinary measures,” or special accounting maneuvers intended to prevent the nation from hitting the debt ceiling, as early as January 14th, in a letter sent to congressional leaders Friday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump says fate of TikTok should be in his hands when he returns to White House
item.description: President-elect Trump said in an amicus brief Friday that he should make the decision as to whether TikTok can continue to operate in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republicans gain edge in Minnesota House after DFLer drops out over residency case
item.description: Republicans gain one-seat majority heading into 2025 session as a DFL-won seat becomes vacant. A special election will come early next year.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: US sanctions the founder of Georgia's ruling political party
item.description: President Joe Biden has imposed sanctions on Bidzina Ivanishvili, the founder of Georgia’s ruling political party.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Baja to lease warehouse space to house deported migrants
item.description: He also stated the warehouse location will have easier access from the ports of entry where migrants might be deported instead of having to bus them to several areas in the city.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, Fox 5

item.title: Manchin objects to Biden's commutations
item.description: Outgoing Sen. Joe Manchin (I-W.Va.) is denouncing as “misguided” and “disgusting” President Biden's move to commute the sentences of two men convicted of murdering a student at Marshall University.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: How Trump changed America’s legal landscape
item.description: President-elect Trump's legal issues dominated his 2024 presidential bid, where courtroom appearances were just as common as rallies and campaign stops.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New 2025 laws hit hot topics from AI in movies to rapid-fire guns
item.description: New laws taking effect with the new year will affect Hollywood actors, social media stars and chatty motorists.
item.dc:creator: David A. Lieb, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: President Biden signs 50 bills into law
item.description: Before leaving to spend the rest of the holiday in the Virgin Islands, President Joe Biden signed 50 bills into law on Christmas Eve, as his time in office comes to an end.
item.dc:creator: Traci Caldwell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota Democratic party chair says his wins could help nationally after loss to Trump
item.description: Ken Martin helped turn around the Minnesota Democratic-Farmer-Labor Party's finances and secure a streak of statewide wins for the party over 18 years. Now, he wants to do the same thing for the Democratic National Committee.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Trump faces roadblocks to ambitious border crackdown
item.description: President-elect Trump has made ambitious promises on the border and immigration, including the biggest deportation operation ever seen. But much like in Trump’s first term in office, his immigration agenda faces significant roadblocks.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Opinion: Elon Musk wants to ‘delete’ many Americans' financial lifeline
item.description: It is especially surprising that one of the first federal agencies to come under scrutiny from the incoming administration is one that has returned billions of dollars to many of the same consumers who were counting on leaders in Washington to look out for their wallets.
item.dc:creator: Sharon McGowan, opinion contributor, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bill Clinton is out of the hospital after being treated for the flu
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Former President Bill Clinton was discharged from a Washington hospital on Tuesday and will be home for Christmas after he was admitted the day before with a fever.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden signs $895B Defense bill despite transgender care provision
item.description: President Biden on Monday evening signed the $895 billion Defense authorization bill, despite a provision included in the massive package that restricts transgender care.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: As costs rise, number of Minnesotans losing heat, electricity spikes
item.description: The Citizens Utility Board of Minnesota, a nonprofit that advocates for utility customers, tracks reported shutoffs. It found utilities disconnected more than 85,000 residential households from either gas or electric services from January through October.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Biden vetoes once-bipartisan effort to add 66 federal judgeships, citing 'hurried' House action
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Monday vetoed a once-bipartisan effort to add 66 federal district judgeships, saying “hurried action” by the House left important questions unanswered about the life-tenured positions.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden signs defense bill despite objections to ban on transgender health care for military children
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Monday signed into law a defense bill that authorizes significant pay raises for junior enlisted service members, aims to counter China's growing power and boosts overall military spending to $895 billion despite his objections to language stripping coverage
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Inside the Gaetz ethics report, a trove of new details alleging payments for sex and drug use
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The House Ethics Committee's long-awaited report on Matt Gaetz documents a trove of salacious allegations, including sex with an underage girl, that tanked the Florida Republican's bid to lead the Justice Department.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Read: House Ethics report on Matt Gaetz allegations
item.description: Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) had moved earlier in the day to block the release of the report through a lawsuit, arguing that the committee was acting beyond its authority.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Biden didn't remove these 3 inmates from death row
item.description: Those who received the Monday commutations of their sentences saw them go from execution to life with no possibility of parole. However, three “hard cases” were not among the commutations.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Ex-US Rep. Gaetz paid women for sex while in Congress: Ethics report
item.description: Ex-U.S. Rep. Matt Gaetz paid tens of thousands to women for sex and drugs, according to a draft of a House ethics report.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: Trump threatens to try to take back the Panama Canal
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — Donald Trump suggested Sunday that his new administration could try to regain control of the Panama Canal that the United States “foolishly” ceded to its Central American ally, contending that shippers are charged “ridiculous” fees to pass through the vital transportation channel link
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden commutes sentences of 37 federal death row prisoners
item.description: Opponents of the death penalty had urged President Joe Biden to take this step, given the number of executions that took place during President-elect Donald Trump's first term.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram, MPR News

item.title: Twenty years after first proposed, NewRange still vying to build Minnesota’s first copper mine
item.description: Nearly 20 years after it was first proposed to state and federal regulators, the project formerly known as PolyMet Mining — now NewRange Copper Nickel — soldiers on, despite environmental groups’ contention that the project in its current form is dead.
item.dc:creator: Dan Kraker, MPR News
